dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4136 IS: Sentencing Commission Improvements Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Booker (for himself and Mr. Durbin) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To include a Federal defender as a nonvoting member of the United States Sentencing Commission.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Sentencing Commission Improvements Act.

section.header: Federal defender as a nonvoting member of the United States Sentencing Commission

subsection.text: Section 991(a) of title 28, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the first sentence, by striking one nonvoting member and inserting 2 nonvoting members; and

in the fifth sentence, by striking shall be an ex officio, nonvoting member and inserting and a Federal public defender, or a community defender, designated by the Defender Services Office of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts, shall be ex officio, nonvoting members.

subsection.text: Section 235(b)(5) of the Sentencing Reform Act of 1984 (18 U.S.C. 3551 note) is amended, in the third sentence, by striking nine members, including two and inserting 10 members, including 3.
